storyAcceptanceCriteria: AC1: The user can navigate to the product listing page
AC2: The product list is displayed with at least product name and basic product information
AC3: The product list loads within an acceptable time frame
AC4: When no products are available, an appropriate empty state message is shown
AC5: The product list is scrollable when items exceed the visible viewport
AC6: Each product item in the list is visually distinct and readable
AC7: The product list is accessible and usable on both desktop and mobile viewports
AC8: The product list reflects the current state of available products

testCases.title: Display product list with multiple products available
testCases.description: Verify that the product listing page loads and displays a list of products when multiple products exist in the system
preconditions: User is authenticated and logged in
At least 3 products exist in the database
User is on the application home page
steps: Navigate to the product listing page via the main navigation menu or direct URL
Wait for the page to fully load
Observe the product list area
testCases.expectedResult: The product listing page is displayed showing all available products, each with at least a product name and basic information visible. The number of products shown matches the number of products in the system.
testCases.automationReason: Stable UI behavior with verifiable DOM elements and product count assertions in Playwright

testCases.title: Product list loads within acceptable time threshold
testCases.description: Verify that the product listing page loads and renders within a maximum acceptable time (e.g., 3 seconds) under normal conditions
preconditions: User is authenticated and logged in
At least 10 products exist in the database
Network is on a standard broadband connection (no throttling)
Browser cache is cleared
steps: Open browser developer tools and go to the Network tab
Navigate to the product listing page
Record the total page load time from the Network tab
Observe when product list items become visible on screen
testCases.expectedResult: The product list page fully loads and products are visible within 3 seconds. The network requests complete without timeout errors.
testCases.automationReason: Playwright can measure page load timing and assert against threshold values using performance APIs

testCases.title: Empty state displayed when no products are available
testCases.description: Verify that an appropriate empty state message is shown when there are no products in the system
preconditions: User is authenticated and logged in
There are zero products in the database
User is on the application home page
steps: Navigate to the product listing page via the main navigation menu or direct URL
Wait for the page to fully load
Observe the product list area
testCases.expectedResult: An empty state message is displayed (e.g., 'No products available' or similar) instead of a blank page or error. No broken layout elements are visible.
testCases.automationReason: Empty state message presence and text can be asserted deterministically in Playwright

testCases.title: Product list is scrollable when items exceed viewport height
testCases.description: Verify that the user can scroll through the product list when the number of products exceeds the visible viewport area
preconditions: User is authenticated and logged in
More than 20 products exist in the database to overflow the viewport
User is on the product listing page
steps: Navigate to the product listing page
Wait for the page to fully load
Note the last visible product item without scrolling
Scroll down using the mouse wheel or scrollbar
Continue scrolling to the bottom of the list
Note the products visible after scrolling
testCases.expectedResult: The page or list container is scrollable. Products that were not visible in the initial viewport become visible after scrolling. All products are reachable by scrolling.
testCases.automationReason: Playwright supports scroll actions and visibility assertions for elements below the fold

testCases.title: Each product item is visually distinct and readable
testCases.description: Verify that each product in the list has clear visual separation and the product information is legible
preconditions: User is authenticated and logged in
At least 5 products exist in the database
User is on the product listing page
steps: Navigate to the product listing page
Wait for the page to fully load
Visually inspect the list for clear separation between product items (borders, cards, spacing)
Read the product name and description for each visible item
Check that text is not truncated in a way that hides critical information
Check that images (if any) are rendered and not broken
testCases.expectedResult: Each product item has a clear visual boundary from adjacent items. Text is readable and not overlapping. Product names and key information are fully visible or truncated with an affordance (e.g., ellipsis with tooltip).
testCases.automationReason: Visual distinctiveness and readability require human judgment; automated visual regression tools could supplement but not replace manual inspection for this criterion

testCases.title: Product list is displayed correctly on desktop viewport
testCases.description: Verify that the product listing page renders correctly on a standard desktop screen resolution
preconditions: User is authenticated and logged in
At least 3 products exist in the database
Browser is set to 1920x1080 or 1280x800 desktop viewport
steps: Open the browser at 1280x800 resolution
Navigate to the product listing page
Wait for the page to fully load
Observe the layout of the product list
Check that no horizontal scrollbar appears unnecessarily
Check that product items are aligned and well-structured
testCases.expectedResult: Product list is rendered correctly in a desktop layout. All product items are visible and properly aligned. No layout overflow or broken styles are observed.
testCases.automationReason: Playwright can set viewport size and assert layout elements are visible and properly positioned

testCases.title: Product list is displayed correctly on mobile viewport
testCases.description: Verify that the product listing page renders correctly and is usable on a mobile screen size
preconditions: User is authenticated and logged in
At least 3 products exist in the database
Browser is set to a mobile viewport (e.g., 375x667 - iPhone SE)
steps: Open the browser or use developer tools to set viewport to 375x667
Navigate to the product listing page
Wait for the page to fully load
Observe that the product list renders in a mobile-friendly layout
Scroll through the list on mobile viewport
Check that touch-friendly tap targets are appropriately sized
testCases.expectedResult: Product list is displayed in a responsive mobile layout. Products are stacked vertically and readable. No elements are clipped or overflowing off-screen. Scrolling works naturally.
testCases.automationReason: Playwright can emulate mobile viewports and devices to verify responsive layout behavior
